title: feat: add @pollinations/ui browser bundle
author: ElliotEtag
pr_body_excerpt:
Adds a browser IIFE build for the root `@pollinations/ui` entry so primitives + tokens load via a plain `<script>` tag in no-build pages (standalone HTML, CodePen, prototypes).

- Root-only IIFE tsup build exposing `window.PollinationsUI`; existing ESM/CJS entries unchanged
- Keeps React/ReactDOM external via `globalThis` shims (incl. `react/jsx-runtime` and `react-dom` `createPortal` for Ark Portal) — no bundled React
- Adds `unpkg`/`jsdelivr` + `./umd` export; ships `styles.css` alongside
- Documents React 19 CDN setup (esm.sh; React 19 dropped UMD) with reliable script ordering
- SDK-backed subpaths stay ESM/CJS only

Verified: build, typecheck, tests, biome; bundle has no `Dynamic require` / no bundled React; real no-build page renders and exercises the portal path.

Fixes #11574

gist.headline: The script tag returns
gist.blurb: We've added a browser-ready bundle for @pollinations/ui. Now you can drop our components straight into a plain HTML file or CodePen with a simple script tag—no bundler required.
gist.summary: Added a browser IIFE build for @pollinations/ui that exposes window.PollinationsUI, enabling CDN usage via unpkg or jsdelivr without bundling React.
gist.impact: Developers can now prototype with Pollinations UI components directly in standalone HTML files, CodePens, or other no-build environments.
gist.visual_concept: Polly the bee wearing a tiny construction hard hat, placing a glowing '<script>' tag block onto a retro computer monitor. Represents building without complex tools (no-build/script tag).
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of Polly the bee wearing a tiny yellow hard hat, carefully placing a glowing '<script>' tag block onto a retro computer monitor. The monitor screen glows softly. Cozy 8-bit pixel art aesthetic. Soft lime green (#ecf874) and pastel gradient background. Chunky retro sprites. Warm lighting, lo-fi vibes like Stardew Valley. Nostalgic but beautiful, emotionally warm.
